notes: US White House executive documents including Executive Orders, OMB Memoranda, National Security Memoranda, and National Strategies. Multiple ID patterns for different document types.

data.description: US Presidential Executive Orders identified by sequential number.
data.notes: Executive Orders have the force of law but can be revoked by subsequent administrations. URLs vary by administration — no stable URL template exists across administrations.

examples.note: Executive Order on Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy AI (Oct 2023)
Executive Order on Improving the Nation's Cybersecurity (May 2021)

data.description: Office of Management and Budget memoranda that guide federal agency implementation of policy.
data.notes: OMB memos use M-YY-NN format (fiscal year and sequence number). They provide binding guidance to federal agencies on policy implementation.

data.description: Presidential directives on national security matters.
data.notes: National Security Memoranda address classified and national security matters. Some are partially or fully classified. Numbering restarts with each administration.

data.description: National strategies identified by topic area and year.
data.notes: National strategies set multi-year policy direction. They are not legally binding but guide agency priorities and budget allocation.
